notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Viper, which the field rarely builds (0% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Rampage (it never builds Overlord, Cursed Scroll) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Krix: +18% on your effective HP at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Rock & Awe
augments.note: damage scales with throw distance; positional

augments.name: The Rumbling
augments.note: refund-on-damage loop with damage/mana/self-slow penalties; net value depends on uptime loops, not mappable

augments.name: Tiny Titan
augments.note: conditional ult form trade (AS for +45% damage taken); risk profile outside the sim

metaBuilds.title: Physical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 25 — about 227 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +34% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Overlord (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 4,395 effective HP vs magical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Physical Bruiser
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 463 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +53% more one-combo burst: try Viper over Overlord (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 17% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 22% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kallari's main threat is Guillotine; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Tiny Titan” ⇒ on-hit; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field jungle 57.8% over 987 games, magnitude not simulated.
